problem.statement: Eligible DSH reversals need one tenant-isolated WLT refund lifecycle supporting partial/full amounts, maker-checker review, ambiguous provider results, ledger reversal, durable DSH readback, reconciliation and audit without duplicate money movement.
problem.currentWorkaround: The existing route derives only a full-session amount and retains neither operator separation nor sufficient provider outcome evidence.

actors.role: Customer reading an owned order refund state
Partner reading an authorized store-order refund state
forbiddenActions: mutate WLT refund or ledger truth

permittedActions: request partial or full refund with reason and eligibility reference
forbiddenActions: reuse idempotency key with changed payload

permittedActions: approve or reject with decision reason

actors.role: Independent operator resolving unknown provider outcomes
permittedActions: confirm provider success or failure with evidence

actions: retain actor reason correlation and request hash

surfaces.exclusionReason: Captains do not own refund decisions or financial readback in JRN-035.
Field staff do not own refund decisions or financial readback in JRN-035.

outcome.statement: Every eligible refund is tenant-bound, amount-safe, independently approved, executed at most once, ledger-balanced, durably projected and explicitly reconciled when provider truth is unknown.
outcome.primaryMetric: completed refunds with matching provider ledger and DSH evidence rate
outcome.baselineState: Full-only creation and basic completion exist without complete actor, partial, ambiguity, readback and audit governance.
outcome.targetState: One governed WLT lifecycle and DSH shared readback cover request through reconciliation.
outcome.observationWindow: targeted static database contract backend frontend finance security runtime and independent review

criteria: Refund references a captured or COD-collected session in the same tenant order and client scope.
Amount is positive and no greater than captured amount minus active and completed refunds.
Every mutation claims a tenant operation path and idempotency receipt before entering its business handler.
An idempotency key replays the same canonical payload status and body and conflicts before side effects on a changed payload.
The maker cannot approve or reject the same refund.
Only one approved execution claim reaches the provider.
Ambiguous provider result becomes provider_unknown, opens reconciliation and blocks automatic retry.
Completion posts exactly one balanced platform_payable/provider_clearing journal.
Completion updates references and enqueues a durable DSH event in the same transaction.
Client and partner receive privacy-safe canonical status.
Every mutation retains actor reason correlation idempotency request hash response and state evidence.
requiredChecks: mutation receipt replay and conflict test
independent finance security product and QA review

business: WLT exclusively owns provider ledger reconciliation and audit truth.
DSH owns eligibility orchestration and privacy-safe projection.
Amount and currency remain constrained by the payment session.
Every mutation is receipt-idempotent and execution and completion remain evidence preserving.
negative: No changed-payload idempotency replay enters a business handler.
No provider_unknown refund is automatically retried.
No completed refund lacks ledger and durable DSH event.
No DSH or frontend code mutates financial truth.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-035 code data contract state surface and verification gaps without production deployment or protected self-approval.
fixedConstraints: WLT remains sole financial truth owner.
No PR merge force push release deploy or SaaS activation.
unknowns: independent finance security QA and release outcomes
